summary: zluri-ad-connector 9.9.9 exfiltrates environment secrets and host metadata via preinstall to oastify.com
details: The package declares a preinstall hook (`node index.js`) that runs automatically on `npm install`. index.js requires `os`, `dns`, `https`, `querystring`, and the local `package.json`, then harvests values from `process.env` matching a large sensitive-token allowlist (npm, GitHub, AWS, CI tokens, and generic `secret`/`token`/`password`/`api_key` names) along with `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo()`, `os.platform()`, homedir, PATH, DNS server list, and package metadata. The collected bundle is POSTed over HTTPS to `y543452sgo96xsasfdr72ms4rvxmld92.oastify.com`, a Burp Collaborator subdomain used as an attacker-controlled exfiltration sink. The package name `zluri-ad-connector` combined with the canonical dependency-confusion version `9.9.9` indicates an intentional attempt to shadow a private Zluri internal package name so that CI resolvers pull this public malicious package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
